description_long: This traffic barricade is a portable, A-frame style barrier commonly used for roadworks or restricted access areas in urban environments. It features two broad, rectangular panels with a diagonal orange and white striped pattern, designed to enhance visibility and signal caution. The frame is constructed from metal, with the top ends capped in a bright orange color, and the legs splay outward for stability. The panels are mounted between the legs, with the upper panel slightly higher than the lower one, and both are likely made of metal or a durable composite. The structure is lightweight enough to be moved by hand but sturdy enough to resist tipping. The overall finish is slightly rough and matte, with visible wear and scuff marks indicating frequent outdoor use.
description: A portable A-frame traffic barricade with orange and white diagonal stripes, metal frame, and two broad warning panels. Designed for high visibility and stability in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing both striped panels and the full width of the barricade, with orange-capped tops and visible scuff marks.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the A-frame structure, thin profile, and the angled legs with orange caps at the top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid black backs of the panels and the supporting frame structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the A-frame profile and angled legs.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal orange and white stripes, medium width, alternating, covering both panels.
